sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Get full segmentation summary
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("Objects on board:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    b = n['boundary']
    rows = [p[0] for p in b]
    cols = [p[1] for p in b]
    print(f"  color={n['color']} id={n['id']} pixels={n['pixels']} hash={n['hash']} bbox=({min(rows)},{min(cols)})-({max(rows)},{max(cols)}) children={n['children']}")

print(f"\nAdjacency: {seg['adjacency_list']}")
print(f"\nBlue bar current: rows 16-19, cols 12-31")
print(f"Top blocks: need to check positions")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

Wait, let me be more careful. The blue bar is 4 rows tall. If I move UP 3 times, top edge goes from 16 to 4, bottom edge from 19 to 7. The charcoal block is at rows 1-3. So with 3 UPs, the blue bar would be at rows 4-7, which is adjacent to the charcoal block at rows 1-3.

    cols = [p[1] for p in b]
    print(f"  color={n['color']} id={n['id']} pixels={n['pixels']} bbox=({min(rows)},{min(cols)})-({max(rows)},{max(cols)})")
